source_anchor: Friedrich Nietzsche, Also sprach Zarathustra, Zarathustra's Vorrede 1, paragraph-aligned twelve-segment trilingual entry through the first prologue movement

chunks.source_anchor: Friedrich Nietzsche, Also sprach Zarathustra, Zarathustra's Vorrede 1, paragraph-aligned twelve-segment trilingual entry through the first prologue movement

witnesses.text: Als Zarathustra dreissig Jahr alt war, verliess er seine Heimat und den See seiner Heimat und gieng in das Gebirge. Hier genoss er seines Geistes und seiner Einsamkeit und wurde dessen zehn Jahre nicht müde. Endlich aber verwandelte sich sein Herz, — und eines Morgens stand er mit der Morgenröthe auf, trat vor die Sonne hin und sprach zu ihr also:

Когда Заратустре исполнилось тридцать лет,
он оставил свою родину и озеро своей родины — 
и ушёл в горы.
Здесь он вкушал свой дух и своё одиночество 
и в течении десяти лет не уставал от этого.
И всё же наконец преобразилось его сердце —;
и однажды утром он встал с самой утренней зарёй,
вышел перед солнцем и так говорил к нему:

When Zarathustra was thirty years old, he left his homeland and the lake of his homeland and went into the mountains. Here he delighted in his spirit and his solitude, and for ten years did not grow weary of them. But at last his heart transformed itself, — and one morning he rose with the dawn, stepped before the sun, and spoke thus to it:

translation_tension.note: 'Einsamkeit' is kept as chosen solitude rather than accidental loneliness, and 'verwandelte sich sein Herz' remains an inner transformation rather than a mere change of mood.

chunks.source_anchor: Friedrich Nietzsche, Also sprach Zarathustra, Zarathustra's Vorrede 1, paragraph-aligned twelve-segment trilingual entry through the first prologue movement

witnesses.text: „Du grosses Gestirn! Was wäre dein Glück, wenn du nicht Die hättest, welchen du leuchtest!

“Ты, великое светило!
Что бы стало твоим счастьем,
если бы у тебя не было тех, кому ты светишь!
“You great star! What would your happiness be, if you did not have those to whom you shine!

translation_tension.note: 'Gestirn' is kept broad enough to preserve star and luminary valence; the witness layers should not reduce the image to a merely astronomical label.

chunks.source_anchor: Friedrich Nietzsche, Also sprach Zarathustra, Zarathustra's Vorrede 1, paragraph-aligned twelve-segment trilingual entry through the first prologue movement

witnesses.text: Zehn Jahre kamst du hier herauf zu meiner Höhle: du würdest deines Lichtes und dieses Weges satt geworden sein, ohne mich, meinen Adler und meine Schlange.

Десять лет ты поднималось ко мне, к моей пещере:
ты пресытилось бы своим светом и этим путём,
если бы не я,
мой орёл и моя змея.
For ten years you came up here to my cave: you would have grown sated with your light and this path, without me, my eagle, and my serpent.

chunks.source_anchor: Friedrich Nietzsche, Also sprach Zarathustra, Zarathustra's Vorrede 1, paragraph-aligned twelve-segment trilingual entry through the first prologue movement

witnesses.text: Aber wir warteten deiner an jedem Morgen, nahmen dir deinen Überfluss ab und segneten dich dafür.

Но мы каждое утро выжидали тебя,
принимали от тебя твой избыток
и благословляли тебя за это.”
But we awaited you every morning, relieved you of your overflow, and blessed you for it.

translation_tension.note: 'Überfluss' is treated as overflow available for reciprocal reception; the movement remains mutual blessing rather than one-sided illumination.

chunks.source_anchor: Friedrich Nietzsche, Also sprach Zarathustra, Zarathustra's Vorrede 1, paragraph-aligned twelve-segment trilingual entry through the first prologue movement

witnesses.text: Siehe! Ich bin meiner Weisheit überdrüssig, wie die Biene, die des Honigs zu viel gesammelt hat,
Behold! I am weary of my wisdom, like the bee that has gathered too much honey;

chunks.source_anchor: Friedrich Nietzsche, Also sprach Zarathustra, Zarathustra's Vorrede 1, paragraph-aligned twelve-segment trilingual entry through the first prologue movement

witnesses.text: ich bedarf der Hände, die sich ausstrecken. Ich möchte verschenken und austheilen, bis die Weisen unter den Menschen wieder einmal ihrer Thorheit und die Armen wieder einmal ihres Reichthums froh geworden sind.

я нуждаюсь в руках, которые простираются;
я хотел бы дарить и раздавать,
пока мудрые среди людей
снова не будут радоваться своей глупости,
а бедные — своему богатству.
I need outstretched hands. I would bestow and distribute, until the wise among men have once again become glad of their folly, and the poor once again of their wealth.

translation_tension.note: 'Thorheit' remains philosophically charged as folly with an edge toward madness; the route should not flatten it into mere stupidity or comic error.

chunks.source_anchor: Friedrich Nietzsche, Also sprach Zarathustra, Zarathustra's Vorrede 1, paragraph-aligned twelve-segment trilingual entry through the first prologue movement

witnesses.text: Dazu muss ich in die Tiefe steigen: wie du des Abends thust, wenn du hinter das Meer gehst und noch der Unterwelt Licht bringst, du überreiches Gestirn!

Для этого я должен спуститься в глубину,
как ты делаешь это вечером,
когда ты уходишь за море
и ещё подземному миру приносишь свет,
ты, сверхизобильная звезда!
For that I must descend into the deep: as you do in the evening when you go behind the sea and still bring light to the underworld, you over-rich star!

translation_tension.note: 'Unterwelt' remains the underworld of downward address and light-bearing descent rather than an abstract elsewhere or neutral distance.

chunks.source_anchor: Friedrich Nietzsche, Also sprach Zarathustra, Zarathustra's Vorrede 1, paragraph-aligned twelve-segment trilingual entry through the first prologue movement

witnesses.text: Ich muss, gleich dir, *untergehen*, wie die Menschen es nennen, zu denen ich hinab will.

Я должен, подобно тебе, *закатиться*,
как называют это люди,
к которым я хочу спуститься.
I must, like you, *go under*, as men call it, to go down to them.

translation_tension.note: 'untergehen' is preserved as down-going or going-under; Russian 'закатиться' keeps the solar and recurrent motion without reducing the act to defeat alone.

chunks.source_anchor: Friedrich Nietzsche, Also sprach Zarathustra, Zarathustra's Vorrede 1, paragraph-aligned twelve-segment trilingual entry through the first prologue movement

witnesses.text: So segne mich denn, du ruhiges Auge, das ohne Neid auch ein allzugrosses Glück sehen kann!

Так благослови же меня, ты спокойное око,
которое без зависти смотрит даже на слишком большое счастье!
So bless me then, you tranquil eye, that can behold even an all-too-great happiness without envy!

chunks.source_anchor: Friedrich Nietzsche, Also sprach Zarathustra, Zarathustra's Vorrede 1, paragraph-aligned twelve-segment trilingual entry through the first prologue movement

witnesses.text: Segne den Becher, welcher überfliessen will, dass das Wasser golden aus ihm fliesse und überallhin den Abglanz deiner Wonne trage!

Благослови чашу,
которая хочет перелиться,
чтобы золото из неё текло
и повсюду несло отблеск твоего ликования!
Bless the cup that wants to overflow, so that the water may flow golden from it and carry everywhere the reflection of your bliss!

translation_tension.note: 'Abglanz' remains afterglow, reflection, or reflected radiance; witness layers should keep the difference between source light and carried shine. 'Wonne' and 'ликование' keep the image affectively high without collapsing into mere pleasure.

chunks.source_anchor: Friedrich Nietzsche, Also sprach Zarathustra, Zarathustra's Vorrede 1, paragraph-aligned twelve-segment trilingual entry through the first prologue movement

witnesses.text: Siehe! Dieser Becher will wieder leer werden, und Zarathustra will wieder Mensch werden.“

Смотри!
Эта чаша хочет вновь стать пустой,
и Заратустра хочет вновь стать человеком.”
Behold! This cup wants to become empty again, and Zarathustra wants to become man again.”

translation_tension.note: 'wieder Mensch werden' is held as a return into the human after ripening, not as simple regression to a prior self; here return stays an action, while eternal return remains only a hidden rhythm of the route.

chunks.source_anchor: Friedrich Nietzsche, Also sprach Zarathustra, Zarathustra's Vorrede 1, paragraph-aligned twelve-segment trilingual entry through the first prologue movement

witnesses.text: — Also begann Zarathustra's Untergang.
– Так начался закат Заратустры.
— Thus began Zarathustra’s going-under.
